items.title: RT by @POTUS: "I have just been informed by our Great Military that last night the Iranians shot down one of our highly sophisticated Apache Helicopters while patrolling over the Strait of Hormuz. There were two pilots involved, both are safe and uninjured. Nevertheless, the United States must, of necessity, respond to this attack." - President Donald J. Trump 🇺🇸

Stocks Join Bonds Higher as Oil Falls Below $90
items.summary:
A rebound on Wall Street powered ahead, with stocks joining bonds higher as oil fell amid hopes the US and Iran are getting closer to a deal to end the war that has rattled global markets.
Liz Ann Sonders, Chief Investment Strategist at Charles Schwab, discusses her midyear outlook as the market drives higher. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: EU considers suspending rise in Russian oil price cap
items.summary: Limit on Russian crude was set to increase from $44 a barrel to $70 following supply disruption due to Middle East war

items.title:
The ideal solution in peace negotiations is to end the war immediately. At the very least, the first step must be taken – an unconditional, full ceasefire. This is important.

A ceasefire is also a complex process. Yes, it is about political will and decisions, but at the same time, today there are kill zones of 20, 30, 40 kilometers. If there are no guarantors and no monitoring of the ceasefire, Russia may take advantage of this and push forward by 20 kilometers in some parts of Ukraine. This is a major risk. That is why we say: a ceasefire must be unconditional.

For a ceasefire to be achieved, it would be better to have a leaders’ meeting – Ukraine, Russia, certainly Europe, and the United States. 

Ukraine has the will for all of this. We’ll see whether Russia has such will as well. So far, they have not demonstrated it.

From answers to journalists’ questions during a joint press conference with President of Estonia Alar Karis (2/2).

items.title: Treasury Market Signals Rates Need to Be Higher
items.summary:
The $31 trillion Treasury market has an unequivocal message for Kevin Warsh’s Federal Reserve: Interest rates aren’t high enough. 
The reset upwards only intensified last week after the latest read on job growth topped all forecasts, reinforcing a growing conviction that rates need to rise in order to rein in inflation pressures and temper the risk of an AI-induced boom overheating the economy.

items.title: Driven by AI demand, China on track to pass US as top nuclear energy producer
items.summary:
China is expected to overtake the United States as the world’s nuclear power leader, as the AI boom and war in the Middle East renew a global push for reliable energy sources, according to a new report.
While the US maintained the world’s largest operational fleet of nuclear reactors, China now accounted for nearly half of all those under construction globally and was expected to match American ca

items.title: Kuwait Oil Chief Seeks Pipeline Alternatives to Skirt Hormuz
items.summary: Kuwait is seeking pipeline alternatives to export its crude oil as the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz starves the market of vital barrels and Persian Gulf producers of the cash to run their economies.

items.title: Inflation Pressures Rise in Asia Despite Fuel Price Caps
items.summary: Deepali Bhargava, ING Head of Research and Chief Economist for APAC, discussed the current inflation dynamics in Asia in the context of recent geopolitical developments, including a fragile ceasefire and renewed tensions in the Middle East. She highlighted that growth across Asia has remained stronger than expected, supported in part by the tech export sector. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Sigma Court Victory Eases Uncertainty Around Lithium Expansion
items.summary: Sigma Lithium Corp. said it won an appeal in Brazil that overturned a lower-court ruling tied to environmental complaints at its sole lithium mine, easing uncertainty as the company pursues a major expansion.

items.title: 'Please send help': Ship's distress call after being hit by US missile
items.summary: The Indian crew of a sanctioned oil tanker urged authorities to "send help" after the ship was hit by a US missile off Oman on Monday, saying it was on fire and sinking.

RT by @UN: I'm deeply alarmed by the renewed escalation in the Middle East.

All attacks must stop immediately.

The ceasefires in Lebanon, Iran & Gaza must be fully respected.

Any steps that could undermine ongoing diplomatic efforts must be avoided.

I'm also deeply concerned by Israel's decision to close crossings into Gaza & reiterate my call for the immediate reopening of all crossings to ensure the rapid, safe & unhindered passage of humanitarian assistance at scale throughout Gaza.

The exercise of navigational rights & freedoms, in accordance with international law, must be respected.

Everyone must uphold their obligations under international law & take all feasible precautions to protect civilians.

There is no military solution to the conflicts in the Middle East.

The only way forward is through dialogue & negotiations.

I urge all concerned to work towards diplomatic solutions that advance regional & international peace & security.

items.title: Kenya Retains Benchmark Interest Rate for Second Time
items.summary: Kenya’s central bank left its benchmark interest rate unchanged for the second time in a row, as it monitors the impact of the ongoing conflict in Iran on the economy.

items.title: ❗️Армия Тайваня проводит учения по отражению атаки китайских войскКак пишет Reuters, маневры  [...]
items.summary: ❗️Армия Тайваня проводит учения по отражению атаки китайских войскКак пишет Reuters, маневры проходят одновременно на восьми участках западного побережья, обращенного к Китаю через Тайваньский пролив. Эта территория рассматривается как наиболее вероятное направление возможной высадки десанта.В ходе учений применяются реактивные системы залпового огня, артиллерия, минометы и противотанковые ракеты.
